body_txt: People fainting in Real Life when presented with a shocking development is a dire occurrence. When it does happen, it's not just suddenly falling asleep. Extreme onset of emotion from fear or anxiety can actually cause some people to go into a comatose state. This is the reason doctors or emergency personnel often ask the recipient to sit down before breaking bad news, such as the death of a loved one or a scary diagnosis like cancer. Medically, fainting is called "syncope." It's generally caused by the brain not getting enough of a bloodflow to work properly, which in turn may be caused by a number of factors, some harmless and some not-so-harmless. When in doubt, ask your doctor. Fainting in fiction usually takes one of the following forms:       Not to be confused with Non-Lethal K.O. , due to the term "fainting" being occasionally interchanged with getting knocked out thanks to certain games like Pokémon .
